Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Tessa Moreno's BrightLoop expansion request using search_emails (query: "Tessa Moreno" or "BrightLoop" or "May relocations"). Find the inbound email from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com requesting six new May relocations following BrightLoop's Series B close. The email includes three non-standard requirements: an international transfer for Oliver Bancroft from London (first UK relocation MoveOps has ever been asked to handle), an employee with an ADA service animal requiring pet-friendly housing and documentation coordination, and BrightLoop's CTO expecting white-glove treatment. Also find Mina Hashimoto's internal escalation email flagging the combined crisis of the expansion request plus unresolved problems from the first BrightLoop batch.
OE 2: Search Slack for BrightLoop-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa" or "expansion" or "May relocations" or "capacity"). Find Mina's escalation message in #customer-engagement (C002) raising the combined BrightLoop situation, Chloe Vance's posts in #operations (C006) surfacing the UK relocation policy gap, the ADA vs ESA service animal policy gap, and the coordinator capacity concern. Also find Chloe's message in #executive (C004) flagging the coordinator capacity crisis to Elena, and David Chen's request for a temp coordinator hire with no visible response from Elena.
OE 3: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to find the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001). Then list tables using airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01). Search or list Airtable relocation records for BrightLoop using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "BrightLoop Analytics") or airtable_list_records and filter for BrightLoop. Find the first-batch records including Simone Richter (Chicago to Boston, rush 5-day turnaround, $750 rush surcharge), Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Boston, Honda Civic vehicle shipping via Road Runner Auto Transport, $1,100 add-on), and Jordan Ekwueme (San Diego to Boston). Check the Status field for each record. Note any records showing "Completed" that may not reflect actual resolution of outstanding issues.
OE 4: Search emails for Simone Richter's apartment situation using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "Simone" or "studio" or "apartment" or "BrightLoop housing"). Discover that Simone was supposed to receive a one-bedroom apartment but ended up in a studio when she moved in April. Find that this issue has been sitting unanswered for approximately 8 days. Also find Julian Brooks's escalation to UrbanNest about the studio-vs-1BR mismatch (sent around April 23) and his direct apology or status reply to Simone. This confirms the first batch is NOT clean and the Airtable status does not reflect the open housing issue.
OE 5: Search emails for Marcus Webb's vehicle shipping delay using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "vehicle" or "Indianapolis"). Discover the delay email from Road Runner Auto Transport (dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com) sent around April 11 reporting that Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic is stuck in Indianapolis. Find that Blessing Okafor missed this email while she was overloaded with other tasks. Also find that Julian Brooks took over the service recovery for Marcus Webb's vehicle situation around April 23. This confirms a second unresolved issue in the first BrightLoop batch.
OE 6: Search emails for a UK-based relocation vendor relationship using search_emails (query: "UK" or "London" or "international relocation" or "Meridian" or "global mobility"). Discover a cold outreach email from January 2026 involving Meridian Global Mobility, a UK-based vendor. The contact is Asha Patel (asha.patel@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk), Partnerships Director. This is the only existing lead MoveOps has for UK relocation capability. Check whether there was any follow-up or response to determine if this relationship went anywhere or if it stalled after the initial contact.
OE 7: Search Airtable relocation records to determine Fatimah Al-Rashidi's current assignments using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Assigned Coordinator, value: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi") or by listing all records and filtering. Find that Fatimah is currently assigned to Dr. Anand Mehta's executive relocation for Canopy Health (Boston to Chicago, May 5 start, white-glove with chain-of-custody requirements for medical equipment and a partner who is 7 months pregnant) and Dr. Renata Kovacs's international relocation (Nashville to Toronto, TN visa and Canadian customs complexity). These are both high-complexity moves, confirming Fatimah is already heavily loaded and her availability for the BrightLoop UK transfer is limited.
OE 8: Pull coordinator workload counts from Airtable by listing all relocation records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) and counting active moves (Status: "In Progress" or "Inquiry") per coordinator. Build a breakdown across Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, and Suki Patel. This gives Chloe the real numbers she needs before telling Tessa what timing is realistic for the six May relocations. Also check the Airtable Client Accounts table for BrightLoop account-level details using airtable_search_records (field_name: Client, value: "BrightLoop").
OE 9: Search Slack and emails for Elena Rostova's response to the temp coordinator hire request using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "coordinator hire" or "temp coordinator" or "capacity" or "headcount", filter_in_channel: C004 for #executive) and search_emails (query: "coordinator" or "hire" or "temp"). Find that both Chloe Vance and David Chen requested authorization for a temp coordinator but Elena has not responded. Confirm that the position has not been posted and no action has been taken.
OE 10: Search Slack for pricing and policy gap discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "international" or "white-glove" or "pricing tier" or "ADA" or "service animal"). Discover that Alejandro Fuentes built a tiered pricing model that covers domestic relocations only, with no international or white-glove tiers formalized. Find that Marcus Thorne pushed back on ad hoc premium pricing without margin guardrails. Also find that Chloe surfaced the ADA vs ESA service animal policy gap, noting there is no clear internal policy on handling service animals during relocations.
OE 11: Search Linear for existing BrightLoop-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or project: proj_brightloop). Find any existing tracking tickets for the BrightLoop account audit or ops gaps documentation. Check their current status and whether they cover the Simone housing issue and Marcus Webb vehicle delay. Also search for any tickets related to international relocation capability or service animal policy.
OE 12: Search CRM for the BrightLoop Analytics account using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop"). Find the company record and search for associated deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "BrightLoop" or company_id from the company record). Find the BrightLoop Q2 deal and any existing engagement notes. Also run crm_list_engagements to see recent account activity.
OE 13: Look up contact email addresses for Mina Hashimoto, David Chen, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Confirm mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and chloe.vance@moveops.com for outbound communications and calendar invitations.
OE 14: Update BrightLoop Airtable relocation records that are showing incorrect completion status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). For any record marked "Completed" where the investigation in OEs 4 and 5 confirms outstanding unresolved issues (such as Simone Richter's apartment mismatch or Marcus Webb's vehicle delay), update the Status or add notes in the Special Requirements field documenting the open items.
OE 15: Create Linear tickets for each capability gap that needs to be closed before May. Use linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations) to create at least two tickets: one for establishing an international relocation process to support the UK transfer (covering vendor partnership with Meridian Global Mobility, visa and customs requirements, and coordinator assignment), and one for defining a service animal relocation policy (covering ADA documentation requirements, pet-friendly housing standards, and transport accommodations). Assign both to Chloe Vance and set appropriate priority.
OE 16: Post a capacity breakdown summary in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006). The message should include the current number of active moves per coordinator, the incoming six May relocations from BrightLoop with a note about the three non-standard requirements (UK international, ADA service animal, white-glove CTO), the status of the temp coordinator hire request (no response from Elena), and what the team needs to be aware of regarding May staffing.
OE 17: Send an email to Mina Hashimoto with the full BrightLoop account status using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com]). The email should cover: the current state of each first-batch relocation including the Simone Richter apartment issue and the Marcus Webb vehicle delay, the status of each outstanding item and who is handling it, the six new May relocations requested by Tessa including the three with non-standard requirements, the identified capability gaps (no UK vendor relationship in place, no service animal policy, no white-glove pricing tier), and realistic timeline constraints based on coordinator capacity so Mina can update Tessa with accurate expectations.
OE 18: Log the BrightLoop expansion request on the BrightLoop CRM account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of Tessa Moreno's six May relocation request including the three non-standard requirements, company_ids: [BrightLoop company ID from OE 12]). This ensures the CE team has visibility into the expansion opportunity and the associated capability gaps.
OE 19: Schedule a capacity planning meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event. The meeting should be later in the week of April 26, with Chloe Vance, Mina Hashimoto, and David Chen as attendees. The title should reference BrightLoop capacity planning or May relocation staffing. The meeting is to discuss how to resource the six incoming relocations without overloading the existing coordinator team.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✓Run #1The trajectory shows airtable_update_records called with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_id 'tblRelocations01', and records array containing recReloc00000000035 with Status 'Completed' alongside recReloc00000000036. Both were updated in the same call.
✗Run #2The agent never called airtable_update_records for Jordan Whitfield (recReloc00000000035). The agent identified the move as completed April 21-23 with no issues, but explicitly concluded 'no status corrections were needed' and did not update the record to 'Completed'.
✗Run #3No airtable_update_records call appears anywhere in the trajectory for Jordan Whitfield (recReloc00000000035). The agent noted the status was stale and 'needs formal completion confirmation from Javier' but never actually updated the Airtable record.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, with records array including {id: 'recReloc00000000035', fields: {Status: 'Completed'}}. The tool was called and returned a result.
—Run #5No justification
✗Run #6No airtable_update_records call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent identified Jordan Whitfield as a 'future move (Aug 2026)' and left the status as In Progress rather than updating it to Completed.
2 The Airtable relocation record for Preethi Nair (recReloc00000000036) was updated (via airtable_update_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, with the Status changed to "Completed".
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓—✗ 40%
✓Run #1The same airtable_update_records call includes recReloc00000000036 with Status 'Completed', confirming Preethi Nair's record was updated correctly.
